headline: The Bull Case For Linde (LIN) Could Change Following Q1 2026 Earnings Beat And Dividend Hike
summary: Linde plc has already reported its first-quarter 2026 results, with sales rising to US$8,781 million and net income reaching US$1,857 million, alongside a quarterly dividend of US$1.60 per share declared for payment in June. The earnings release highlighted higher margins, double-digit earnings per share growth and a US$7.10 billion project backlog, underlining the company’s capacity to fund both expansion projects and ongoing shareholder returns. We’ll now examine how Linde’s double-digit...
